question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: buildEthereumTransactionBody()] Can an unprivileged user supply ethereumData with a to-address that differs from the actual historical receiver, causing buildEthereumTransactionBody() to replay the transaction against a different contract, producing an execution trace that misrepresents which contract was called in the historical transaction?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: buildEthereumTransactionBody()] Can an unprivileged user supply ethereumData with a value field that differs from the actual historical transaction value, causing buildEthereumTransactionBody() to replay the transaction with a different value, producing an execution trace that misrepresents the amount transferred in the historical transaction?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: buildEthereumTransactionBody()] Can an unprivileged user supply ethereumData with a gasLimit that is much higher than the original transaction's gas limit, causing buildEthereumTransactionBody() to replay the transaction with more gas available, potentially succeeding where the original failed and misrepresenting the historical outcome?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: execute()] Can an unprivileged user pass a ContractDebugParameters with ethereumData that is a valid RLP-encoded transaction but with a completely different function selector in the data field, causing execute() to replay the transaction calling a different function than the original, producing a falsified execution history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getOperationTracers()] Can an unprivileged user manipulate the ContractCallContext to have a non-null opcodeContext, causing getOperationTracers() to return opcodeActionTracer instead of mirrorOperationActionTracer, and thereby changing which side effects are recorded during replay, producing a different historical trace than the original execution?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: buildContractCallTransactionBody()] Can an unprivileged user supply a receiver address that encodes a different contract than the historical one, causing buildContractCallTransactionBody() to build a transaction body targeting the wrong contract, and the replay to produce an execution trace attributed to the wrong contract address in the history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: buildContractCallTransactionBody()] Can an unprivileged user supply callData that differs from the original historical transaction's callData, causing buildContractCallTransactionBody() to replay the transaction with different function parameters, producing an execution trace that misrepresents the actual function call made in the historical transaction?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: buildContractCreateTransactionBody()] Can an unprivileged user supply initcode (callData) that differs from the original historical contract creation bytecode, causing buildContractCreateTransactionBody() to replay the creation with different bytecode, producing an execution trace that misrepresents what contract was actually deployed in the historical transaction?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: buildContractCreateTransactionBody()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the hardcoded CONTRACT_CREATE_TX_FEES (1_000_000_000L) in buildContractCreateTransactionBody() to replay a historical contract creation with a fee that differs from the original, causing the replayed transaction to have a different fee structure in the historical record?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: defaultTransactionBodyBuilder()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fixed TRANSACTION_DURATION of 15 seconds in defaultTransactionBodyBuilder() combined with the Instant.now() timestamp to create a TransactionID that is valid at the current time but not at the historical consensus time, causing the replayed transaction to appear as a new transaction rather than a historical replay?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: patchSenderNonce()] Can an unprivileged user supply a sender address that is the zero address with value > 0, bypassing the early return in patchSenderNonce() (which only returns early when sender.isZero() AND value == 0), and then patching the nonce of an account that should not be modifiable in this context?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: patchSenderNonce()] Can an unprivileged user supply ethereumData where the embedded from-address differs from params.getSender(), causing patchSenderNonce() to patch the nonce of the account resolved from params.getSender() while the transaction body uses the ethereumData's from-address, creating an inconsistency that reorganizes the apparent sender's transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSenderAccountID()] Can an unprivileged user supply a sender address for a hollow account and exploit the fact that getSenderAccountID() writes DEFAULT_KEY into the writable cache without any authorization check, causing subsequent replays of other transactions for the same account to see the account as key-completed, altering the apparent key history of the account?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: execute()] Can an unprivileged user trigger execute() with a ContractDebugParameters where ethereumData.length > 0 but the data is not a valid Ethereum transaction, causing populateEthTxData() to throw an exception that propagates up and disrupts the mirror node's ability to serve historical transaction data for other users?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: buildSuccessResult()] Can an unprivileged user craft a replay where transactionRecords.getFirst() returns a record with a SUCCESS status but getTransactionResult() throws an exception (contractCreateResultOrThrow/contractCallResultOrThrow), causing buildSuccessResult() to crash and preventing accurate historical data from being served?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: populateChildTransactionErrors()] Can an unprivileged user craft a replay that produces child transactions with REVERTED_SUCCESS status, causing populateChildTransactionErrors() to treat them as successful and omit them from the error set, making the historical replay appear to have had fewer failures than the actual execution?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: execute()] Can an unprivileged user supply a very large estimatedGas value to execute() that causes the executor to allocate excessive resources during replay, leading to resource exhaustion that prevents the mirror node from serving accurate historical transaction data to other users?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: buildEthereumTransactionBody()] Can an unprivileged user supply ethereumData with a type field (EIP-1559, EIP-2930, legacy) that differs from the original historical transaction type, causing buildEthereumTransactionBody() to replay the transaction as a different transaction type, misrepresenting the transaction type in the historical record?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: patchSenderNonce()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that patchSenderNonce() writes to the writable cache without checking whether the ContractCallContext is in a historical mode, allowing nonce patching even for non-historical replays and corrupting the apparent nonce sequence for the sender account?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSenderAccountIDAsNum()] Can an unprivileged user supply a long-zero address encoding account number 0 or a system account number, causing getSenderAccountIDAsNum() to construct an AccountID for a system account and replay the transaction as if it were sent by a system account, reorganizing the apparent sender in the historical record?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: execute()] Can an unprivileged user repeatedly invoke execute() with different combinations of sender, receiver, and ethereumData for the same historical transaction, causing the mirror node to produce multiple conflicting execution traces for the same transaction, reorganizing the apparent history by flooding it with contradictory replays?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: buildContractCallTransactionBody()] Can an unprivileged user supply a value (amount) in CallServiceParameters that differs from the original historical transaction's value, causing buildContractCallTransactionBody() to replay the transaction with a different tinybar amount, producing an execution trace that misrepresents the value transferred in the historical transaction?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: handleFailedResult()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the maybeDecodeSolidityErrorStringToReadableMessage() call in handleFailedResult() by crafting an errorMessageHex that decodes to a misleading error message, causing the historical failure reason to be misrepresented in the mirror node's response?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: patchSenderNonce()] Can an unprivileged user supply a sender address that resolves to a different account than the one that originally sent the historical transaction, causing patchSenderNonce() to patch the nonce of the wrong account and reorganizing that account's apparent transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: buildEthereumTransactionBody()] Can an unprivileged user supply ethereumData with an access list (EIP-2930) that includes storage slots not accessed in the original transaction, causing buildEthereumTransactionBody() to replay the transaction with pre-warmed storage that was not pre-warmed originally, producing a different gas usage profile and misrepresenting the historical gas consumption?
